name: Vegetarian Steak with Mushrooms & Bamboo Shoo
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup cloud ears [Similar to wood ears, but white and more expensive. S.C.]
1 teaspoon cornstarch mixed with 1/4 cup water
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the vegetarian steak (Item D) into bite-sized pieces.
2. Wash the bamboo shoots (Item F), soak them for 15 minutes in warm water, drain and cut each piece in quarters.
3. Soak the Chinese mushrooms (Item G) for 15 to 20 minutes in cold water, discard the water, remove any hard tips, and cut each mushroom in half.
4. Place the dried lily flowers (Item H) in a large pan, cover with 1 cup of hot water, soak for 15 to 30 minutes, discard the water, wash the lily flowers well.
5. Heat the peanut oil in a wok or skillet. Stir-fry the scallion and ginger until fragrant.
6. Add the vegetarian steak pieces (Item D) and cook for a few seconds until they start to brown.
7. Add the bamboo shoots (Item F), Chinese mushrooms (Item G), and dried lily flowers (Item H). Stir-fry everything together for 2 minutes.
8. Add the remaining ingredients (sugar, salt, soy sauce, cornstarch-water mixture, sesame oil) to the pan. Stir until all ingredients are well combined.
9. Reduce heat to low and simmer for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the gravy has thickened.
10. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed, then remove from heat and serve over rice or noodles.
This recipe provides a clear, step-by-step guide to preparing a flavorful vegetarian dish inspired by traditional Chinese cuisine, emphasizing the use of mushrooms and bamboo shoots alongside a hearty vegetarian steak option.
